title: Rottlera repanda
intro: Rottlera repanda, also known as Kutki or Kutaja, is a plant species native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Celastraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antioxidant properties, which help to manage fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Rottlera repanda include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Rottlera repanda include decoctions, powder, and tablets, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive disorders.
taxonomy: Rottlera repanda is classified within the family Celastraceae. This plant belongs to the order Celastrales. It is a species of evergreen shrub or small tree native to various regions, including Asia and Australia. The classification of Rottlera repanda is based on its morphological and phylogenetic characteristics. Its classification helps in understanding its evolutionary relationships with other species within the Celastraceae family.
uses: Rottlera repanda, also known as "Kulsi" in Ayurvedic medicine, is used to treat a variety of ailments. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions. In traditional medicine, the leaves and roots of Rottlera repanda are used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is also believed to have antiseptic and antifungal properties, which can help to prevent infections. Additionally, Rottlera repanda is used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat digestive issues and promote overall health.
